preamble: Hardening a glottal stop into an alveolar stop [t] is a process of re-buccalization or fortition, restoring a specific oral place of articulation to a laryngeal sound.

examples.note: Hypercorrection or emphatic speech where glottalized stops are 'hardened' back to alveolar stops.

sources: Trubetzkoy, N. S. (1939). Grundzüge der Phonologie. TCLP 7. Prague. (English: Principles of Phonology, 1969, University of California Press.)
